architecture.summary: TIARA is a knowledge-base question-answering (KBQA) system built on pre-trained language models. It performs multi-grained retrieval to surface relevant KB context at three levels—entities, exemplary logical forms, and schema items—which are injected into the PLM's context. The PLM then generates executable logical forms, with constrained decoding applied to enforce syntactic and semantic correctness and reduce generation errors.

biblio.title: TIARA: Multi-grained Retrieval for Robust Question Answering over Large Knowledge Base

claims.statement: TIARA outperforms previous SOTA on GrailQA by at least 4.1 F1 points
TIARA outperforms previous SOTA on WebQuestionsSP by at least 1.1 F1 points
On GrailQA, TIARA outperforms previous models in all categories, with an improvement of 4.7 F1 points in zero-shot generalization
Multi-grained retrieval helps the PLM focus on the most relevant KB context: entities, exemplary logical forms, and schema items
Constrained decoding is used to control the output space and reduce generation errors
